c74object.name: fluid.bufcompose~
c74object.description: A utility for manipulating the contents of buffers.

discussion.p: This object is a low-level tool for manipulating buffers and their contents. By specifying ranges of samples and channels to copy, as well as destination and source gains it can provide a powerful interface for performing actions such as a Left/Right to Mid/Side conversion and mixing down multichannel audio
Read more about fluid.bufcompose~ on the learn platform.
fluid.bufcompose~ is part of the Fluid Decomposition Toolkit of the FluCoMa project. For more explanations, learning material, and discussions on its musicianly uses, visit flucoma.org.

method.name: bang
method.description: A bang will commence processing on object given its current settings, unless it is already busy. In that case, the behaviour depends on the value of the queue attribute.

method.name: cancel
method.description: When processing in non-blocking mode, this will cancel the job underway, without updating destination buffers.

method.name: reset
method.digest: Return the object to its original state
method.description: Calling reset will change parameter values back to those the object was created with, i.e. any attribute values set in the box will be retained

description.p: The gain applied to the samples in the region of the destination buffer over which the source is to be copied. The default value (0) will overwrite that section of the destination buffer, and a value of 1.0 would sum the source to the material that was present.
The channel offset in the destination buffer to start writing the source at. The destination buffer will be resized if the number of channels to copy is overflowing.
The time offset (in samples) in the destination buffer to start writing the source at. The destination buffer will be resized if the portion to copy is overflowing.
The name of the destination buffer.
The gain applied to the samples to be copied from the source buffer.
The number of channels from which to copy in the source buffer. This parameter will wrap around the number of channels in the source buffer. The default (-1) copies all of the buffer's channels.
The duration (in samples) to copy from the source buffer. The default (-1) copies the full length of the buffer.
The name of the source buffer.
The first channel from which to copy in the source buffer.
The starting point (in samples) from which to copy in the source buffer.
Enable warnings to be issued whenever a parameter value is constrained (e.g. clipped)
